Every test should use this fixture by default to prevent
access to the normal database.

functions.name: plain.models.test.pytest.setup_db

This fixture is called automatically by `db`,
so a test database will only be setup if the `db` fixture is used.

functions.name: plain.models.utils.make_model_tuple
documentation:
Take a model or a string of the form "package_label.ModelName" and return a
corresponding ("package_label", "modelname") tuple. If a tuple is passed in,
assume it's a valid model tuple already and return it unchanged.

functions.name: plain.models.utils.resolve_callables
documentation:
Generate key/value pairs for the given mapping where the values are
evaluated if they're callable.

classes.name: plain.models.utils.AltersData

functions.name: plain.models.test.utils.setup_databases
documentation: Create the test databases.

functions.name: plain.models.test.utils.get_unique_databases_and_mirrors
documentation:
Figure out which databases actually need to be created.

Deduplicate entries in DATABASES that correspond the same database or are
configured as test mirrors.

Return two values:
- test_databases: ordered mapping of signatures to (name, list of aliases)
                  where all aliases share the same underlying database.
- mirrored_aliases: mapping of mirror aliases to original aliases.

functions.name: plain.models.test.utils.teardown_databases
documentation: Destroy all the non-mirror databases.

functions.name: plain.models.test.utils.dependency_ordered
documentation:
Reorder test_databases into an order that honors the dependencies
described in TEST[DEPENDENCIES].

functions.name: plain.models.preflight._check_lazy_references
documentation:
Ensure all lazy (i.e. string) model references have been resolved.

Lazy references are used in various places throughout Plain, primarily in
related fields and model signals. Identify those common cases and provide
more helpful error messages for them.

The ignore parameter is used by StatePackages to exclude swappable models from
this check.

modules.name: plain.models.query_utils
documentation:
Various data structures used in query construction.

Factored out from plain.models.query to avoid making the main module very
large and/or so that they can be used by other modules without getting into
circular import difficulties.

functions.name: plain.models.query_utils.select_related_descend
documentation:
Return True if this field should be used to descend deeper for
select_related() purposes. Used by both the query construction code
(compiler.get_related_selections()) and the model instance creation code
(compiler.klass_info).

Arguments:
 * field - the field to be checked
 * restricted - a boolean field, indicating if the field list has been
   manually restricted using a requested clause)
 * requested - The select_related() dictionary.
 * select_mask - the dictionary of selected fields.
 * reverse - boolean, True if we are checking a reverse select related

functions.name: plain.models.query_utils.refs_expression
documentation:
Check if the lookup_parts contains references to the given annotations set.
Because the LOOKUP_SEP is contained in the default annotation names, check
each prefix of the lookup_parts for a match.

functions.name: plain.models.query_utils.check_rel_lookup_compatibility
documentation:
Check that self.model is compatible with target_opts. Compatibility
is OK if:
  1) model and opts match (where proxy inheritance is removed)
  2) model is parent of opts' model or the other way around

classes.name: plain.models.query_utils.Q

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.Q.flatten
documentation:
Recursively yield this Q object and all subexpressions, in depth-first
order.

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.Q.check
documentation:
Do a database query to check if the expressions of the Q instance
matches against the expressions.

Encapsulate filters as objects that can then be combined logically (using

classes.name: plain.models.query_utils.DeferredAttribute

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.DeferredAttribute.__get__
documentation:
Retrieve and caches the value from the datastore on the first lookup.
Return the cached value.

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.DeferredAttribute._check_parent_chain
documentation:
Check if the field value can be fetched from a parent field already
loaded in the instance. This can be done if the to-be fetched
field is a primary key field.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this
object the first time, the query is executed.

classes.name: plain.models.query_utils.class_or_instance_method
documentation:
Hook used in RegisterLookupMixin to return partial functions depending on
the caller type (instance or class of models.Field).

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.RegisterLookupMixin.merge_dicts
documentation:
Merge dicts in reverse to preference the order of the original list. e.g.,
merge_dicts([a, b]) will preference the keys in 'a' over those in 'b'.

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.RegisterLookupMixin._unregister_class_lookup
documentation:
Remove given lookup from cls lookups. For use in tests only as it's
not thread-safe.

methods.name: plain.models.query_utils.RegisterLookupMixin._unregister_instance_lookup
documentation:
Remove given lookup from instance lookups. For use in tests only as
it's not thread-safe.

QuerySet.annotate() only accepts expression-like arguments

Specify custom filtering in the ON clause of SQL joins.

modules.name: plain.models.sql.constants
documentation: Constants specific to the SQL storage portion of the ORM.

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.add
documentation:
Add 'objs' to the collection of objects to be deleted.  If the call is
the result of a cascade, 'source' should be the model that caused it,
and 'nullable' should be set to True if the relation can be null.

Return a list of all objects that were not already collected.

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.add_field_update
documentation:
Schedule a field update. 'objs' must be a homogeneous iterable
collection of model instances (e.g. a QuerySet).

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.can_fast_delete
documentation:
Determine if the objects in the given queryset-like or single object
can be fast-deleted. This can be done if there are no cascades, no
parents and no signal listeners for the object class.

The 'from_field' tells where we are coming from - we need this to
determine if the objects are in fact to be deleted. Allow also
skipping parent -> child -> parent chain preventing fast delete of
the child.

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.get_del_batches
documentation: Return the objs in suitably sized batches for the used connection.

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.collect
documentation:
Add 'objs' to the collection of objects to be deleted as well as all
parent instances.  'objs' must be a homogeneous iterable collection of
model instances (e.g. a QuerySet).  If 'collect_related' is True,
related objects will be handled by their respective on_delete handler.

If the call is the result of a cascade, 'source' should be the model
that caused it and 'nullable' should be set to True, if the relation
can be null.

If 'reverse_dependency' is True, 'source' will be deleted before the
current model, rather than after. (Needed for cascading to parent
models, the one case in which the cascade follows the forwards
direction of an FK rather than the reverse direction.)

If 'keep_parents' is True, data of parent model's will be not deleted.

If 'fail_on_restricted' is False, error won't be raised even if it's
prohibited to delete such objects due to RESTRICT, that defers
restricted object checking in recursive calls where the top-level call
may need to collect more objects to determine whether restricted ones
can be deleted.

methods.name: plain.models.deletion.Collector.related_objects
documentation: Get a QuerySet of the related model to objs via related fields.

modules.name: plain.models.sql.where
documentation: Code to manage the creation and SQL rendering of 'where' constraints.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.where.WhereNode

methods.name: plain.models.sql.where.WhereNode.split_having_qualify
documentation:
Return three possibly None nodes: one for those parts of self that
should be included in the WHERE clause, one for those parts of self
that must be included in the HAVING clause, and one for those parts
that refer to window functions.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.where.WhereNode.as_sql
documentation:
Return the SQL version of the where clause and the value to be
substituted in. Return '', [] if this node matches everything,
None, [] if this node is empty, and raise EmptyResultSet if this
node can't match anything.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.where.WhereNode.relabel_aliases
documentation:
Relabel the alias values of any children. 'change_map' is a dictionary
mapping old (current) alias values to the new values.

An SQL WHERE clause.

The class is tied to the Query class that created it (in order to create
the correct SQL).

A child is usually an expression producing boolean values. Most likely the
expression is a Lookup instance.

However, a child could also be any class with as_sql() and either
relabeled_clone() method or relabel_aliases() and clone() methods and
contains_aggregate attribute.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.where.NothingNode
documentation: A node that matches nothing.

documentation: Query subclasses which provide extra functionality beyond simple data retrieval.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.DeleteQuery

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.DeleteQuery.delete_batch
documentation:
Set up and execute delete queries for all the objects in pk_list.

More than one physical query may be executed if there are a
lot of values in pk_list.
A DELETE SQL query.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery._setup_query
documentation:
Run on initialization and at the end of chaining. Any attributes that
would normally be set in __init__() should go here instead.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery.add_update_values
documentation:
Convert a dictionary of field name to value mappings into an update
query. This is the entry point for the public update() method on
querysets.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery.add_update_fields
documentation:
Append a sequence of (field, model, value) triples to the internal list
that will be used to generate the UPDATE query. Might be more usefully
called add_update_targets() to hint at the extra information here.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery.add_related_update
documentation:
Add (name, value) to an update query for an ancestor model.

Update are coalesced so that only one update query per ancestor is run.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.UpdateQuery.get_related_updates
documentation:
Return a list of query objects: one for each update required to an
ancestor model. Each query will have the same filtering conditions as
the current query but will only update a single table.
An UPDATE SQL query.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.subqueries.AggregateQuery
documentation:
Take another query as a parameter to the FROM clause and only select the
elements in the provided list.

modules.name: plain.models.sql.datastructures
documentation:
Useful auxiliary data structures for query construction. Not useful outside
the SQL domain.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.datastructures.MultiJoin
documentation:
Used by join construction code to indicate the point at which a
multi-valued join was attempted (if the caller wants to treat that
exceptionally).

classes.name: plain.models.sql.datastructures.Join

methods.name: plain.models.sql.datastructures.Join.as_sql
documentation:
Generate the full
   LEFT OUTER JOIN sometable ON sometable.somecol = othertable.othercol, params
clause for this join.

Used by sql.Query and sql.SQLCompiler to generate JOIN clauses into the
FROM entry. For example, the SQL generated could be
    LEFT OUTER JOIN "sometable" T1
    ON ("othertable"."sometable_id" = "sometable"."id")

This class is primarily used in Query.alias_map. All entries in alias_map
must be Join compatible by providing the following attributes and methods:
    - table_name (string)
    - table_alias (possible alias for the table, can be None)
    - join_type (can be None for those entries that aren't joined from
      anything)
    - parent_alias (which table is this join's parent, can be None similarly
      to join_type)
    - as_sql()
    - relabeled_clone()

classes.name: plain.models.sql.datastructures.BaseTable
documentation:
The BaseTable class is used for base table references in FROM clause. For
example, the SQL "foo" in
    SELECT * FROM "foo" WHERE somecond
could be generated by this class.

modules.name: plain.models.query
documentation: The main QuerySet implementation. This provides the public API for the ORM.

functions.name: plain.models.query.normalize_prefetch_lookups
documentation: Normalize lookups into Prefetch objects.

functions.name: plain.models.query.prefetch_related_objects
documentation:
Populate prefetched object caches for a list of model instances based on
the lookups/Prefetch instances given.

functions.name: plain.models.query.get_prefetcher
documentation:
For the attribute 'through_attr' on the given instance, find
an object that has a get_prefetch_queryset().
Return a 4 tuple containing:
(the object with get_prefetch_queryset (or None),
 the descriptor object representing this relationship (or None),
 a boolean that is False if the attribute was not found at all,
 a function that takes an instance and returns a boolean that is True if
 the attribute has already been fetched for that instance)

functions.name: plain.models.query.prefetch_one_level
documentation:
Helper function for prefetch_related_objects().

Run prefetches on all instances using the prefetcher object,
assigning results to relevant caches in instance.

Return the prefetched objects along with any additional prefetches that
must be done due to prefetch_related lookups found from default managers.

classes.name: plain.models.query.ModelIterable

classes.name: plain.models.query.RawModelIterable

Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(named=True) that yields a

classes.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.__deepcopy__
documentation: Don't populate the QuerySet's cache.
PYTHON MAGIC METHODS #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.__iter__
documentation:
The queryset iterator protocol uses three nested iterators in the
default case:
    1. sql.compiler.execute_sql()
       - Returns 100 rows at time (constants.GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE)
         using cursor.fetchmany(). This part is responsible for
         doing some column masking, and returning the rows in chunks.
    2. sql.compiler.results_iter()
       - Returns one row at time. At this point the rows are still just
         tuples. In some cases the return values are converted to
         Python values at this location.
    3. self.iterator()
       - Responsible for turning the rows into model objects.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.__getitem__
documentation: Retrieve an item or slice from the set of results.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._iterator
documentation: METHODS THAT DO DATABASE QUERIES #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.iterator
documentation:
An iterator over the results from applying this QuerySet to the
database. chunk_size must be provided for QuerySets that prefetch
related objects. Otherwise, a default chunk_size of 2000 is supplied.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.aggregate
documentation:
Return a dictionary containing the calculations (aggregation)
over the current queryset.

If args is present the expression is passed as a kwarg using
the Aggregate object's default alias.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.count
documentation:
Perform a SELECT COUNT() and return the number of records as an
integer.

If the QuerySet is already fully cached, return the length of the
cached results set to avoid multiple SELECT COUNT(*) calls.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.get
documentation:
Perform the query and return a single object matching the given
keyword arguments.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.create
documentation:
Create a new object with the given kwargs, saving it to the database
and returning the created object.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.bulk_create
documentation:
Insert each of the instances into the database. Do *not* call
save() on each of the instances, do not send any pre/post_save
signals, and do not set the primary key attribute if it is an
autoincrement field (except if features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True).
Multi-table models are not supported.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.bulk_update
documentation: Update the given fields in each of the given objects in the database.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.get_or_create
documentation:
Look up an object with the given kwargs, creating one if necessary.
Return a tuple of (object, created), where created is a boolean
specifying whether an object was created.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.update_or_create
documentation:
Look up an object with the given kwargs, updating one with defaults
if it exists, otherwise create a new one. Optionally, an object can
be created with different values than defaults by using
create_defaults.
Return a tuple (object, created), where created is a boolean
specifying whether an object was created.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._extract_model_params
documentation:
Prepare `params` for creating a model instance based on the given
kwargs; for use by get_or_create().

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._earliest
documentation:
Return the earliest object according to fields (if given) or by the
model's Meta.get_latest_by.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.latest
documentation:
Return the latest object according to fields (if given) or by the
model's Meta.get_latest_by.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.first
documentation: Return the first object of a query or None if no match is found.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.last
documentation: Return the last object of a query or None if no match is found.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.in_bulk
documentation:
Return a dictionary mapping each of the given IDs to the object with
that ID. If `id_list` isn't provided, evaluate the entire QuerySet.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.delete
documentation: Delete the records in the current QuerySet.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._raw_delete
documentation:
Delete objects found from the given queryset in single direct SQL
query. No signals are sent and there is no protection for cascades.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.update
documentation:
Update all elements in the current QuerySet, setting all the given
fields to the appropriate values.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._update
documentation:
A version of update() that accepts field objects instead of field names.
Used primarily for model saving and not intended for use by general
code (it requires too much poking around at model internals to be
useful at that level).

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.exists
documentation: Return True if the QuerySet would have any results, False otherwise.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.contains
documentation:
Return True if the QuerySet contains the provided obj,
False otherwise.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.explain
documentation:
Runs an EXPLAIN on the SQL query this QuerySet would perform, and
returns the results.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.raw
documentation: PUBLIC METHODS THAT RETURN A QUERYSET SUBCLASS #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.dates
documentation:
Return a list of date objects representing all available dates for
the given field_name, scoped to 'kind'.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.datetimes
documentation:
Return a list of datetime objects representing all available
datetimes for the given field_name, scoped to 'kind'.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.none
documentation: Return an empty QuerySet.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.all
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet that is a copy of the current one. This allows a
QuerySet to proxy for a model manager in some cases.
PUBLIC METHODS THAT ALTER ATTRIBUTES AND RETURN A NEW QUERYSET #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.filter
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance with the args ANDed to the existing
set.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.exclude
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance with NOT (args) ANDed to the existing
set.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.complex_filter
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance with filter_obj added to the filters.

filter_obj can be a Q object or a dictionary of keyword lookup
arguments.

This exists to support framework features such as 'limit_choices_to',
and usually it will be more natural to use other methods.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.select_for_update
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance that will select objects with a
FOR UPDATE lock.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.select_related
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance that will select related objects.

If fields are specified, they must be ForeignKey fields and only those
related objects are included in the selection.

If select_related(None) is called, clear the list.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.prefetch_related
documentation:
Return a new QuerySet instance that will prefetch the specified
Many-To-One and Many-To-Many related objects when the QuerySet is
evaluated.

When prefetch_related() is called more than once, append to the list of
prefetch lookups. If prefetch_related(None) is called, clear the list.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.annotate
documentation:
Return a query set in which the returned objects have been annotated
with extra data or aggregations.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.alias
documentation: Return a query set with added aliases for extra data or aggregations.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.order_by
documentation: Return a new QuerySet instance with the ordering changed.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.distinct
documentation: Return a new QuerySet instance that will select only distinct results.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.extra
documentation: Add extra SQL fragments to the query.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.reverse
documentation: Reverse the ordering of the QuerySet.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.defer
documentation:
Defer the loading of data for certain fields until they are accessed.
Add the set of deferred fields to any existing set of deferred fields.
The only exception to this is if None is passed in as the only
parameter, in which case removal all deferrals.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.only
documentation:
Essentially, the opposite of defer(). Only the fields passed into this
method and that are not already specified as deferred are loaded
immediately when the queryset is evaluated.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.using
documentation: Select which database this QuerySet should execute against.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.ordered
documentation:
Return True if the QuerySet is ordered -- i.e. has an order_by()
clause or a default ordering on the model (or is empty).
PUBLIC INTROSPECTION ATTRIBUTES #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet.db
documentation: Return the database used if this query is executed now.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._insert
documentation:
Insert a new record for the given model. This provides an interface to
the InsertQuery class and is how Model.save() is implemented.
PRIVATE METHODS #

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._batched_insert
documentation: Helper method for bulk_create() to insert objs one batch at a time.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._chain
documentation:
Return a copy of the current QuerySet that's ready for another
operation.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._clone
documentation:
Return a copy of the current QuerySet. A lightweight alternative
to deepcopy().

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._next_is_sticky
documentation:
Indicate that the next filter call and the one following that should
be treated as a single filter. This is only important when it comes to
determining when to reuse tables for many-to-many filters. Required so
that we can filter naturally on the results of related managers.

This doesn't return a clone of the current QuerySet (it returns
"self"). The method is only used internally and should be immediately
followed by a filter() that does create a clone.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._merge_sanity_check
documentation: Check that two QuerySet classes may be merged.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._merge_known_related_objects
documentation: Keep track of all known related objects from either QuerySet instance.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._add_hints
documentation:
Update hinting information for use by routers. Add new key/values or
overwrite existing key/values.

methods.name: plain.models.query.QuerySet._has_filters
documentation:
Check if this QuerySet has any filtering going on. This isn't
equivalent with checking if all objects are present in results, for
example, qs[1:]._has_filters() -> False.
Represent a lazy database lookup for a set of objects.

classes.name: plain.models.query.EmptyQuerySet
documentation:
Marker class to checking if a queryset is empty by .none():
    isinstance(qs.none(), EmptyQuerySet) -> True

classes.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.resolve_model_init_order
documentation: Resolve the init field names and value positions.

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.prefetch_related
documentation: Same as QuerySet.prefetch_related()

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet._clone
documentation: Same as QuerySet._clone()

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.db
documentation: Return the database used if this query is executed now.

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.using
documentation: Select the database this RawQuerySet should execute against.

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.columns
documentation:
A list of model field names in the order they'll appear in the
query results.

methods.name: plain.models.query.RawQuerySet.model_fields
documentation: A dict mapping column names to model field names.

Provide an iterator which converts the results of raw SQL queries into
annotated model instances.

classes.name: plain.models.query.RelatedPopulator
documentation:
RelatedPopulator is used for select_related() object instantiation.

The idea is that each select_related() model will be populated by a
different RelatedPopulator instance. The RelatedPopulator instances get
klass_info and select (computed in SQLCompiler) plus the used db as
input for initialization. That data is used to compute which columns
to use, how to instantiate the model, and how to populate the links
between the objects.

The actual creation of the objects is done in populate() method. This
method gets row and from_obj as input and populates the select_related()
model instance.

functions.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.cursor_iter
documentation:
Yield blocks of rows from a cursor and ensure the cursor is closed when
done.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.pre_sql_setup
documentation:
Do any necessary class setup immediately prior to producing SQL. This
is for things that can't necessarily be done in __init__ because we
might not have all the pieces in place at that time.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_group_by
documentation:
Return a list of 2-tuples of form (sql, params).

The logic of what exactly the GROUP BY clause contains is hard
to describe in other words than "if it passes the test suite,
then it is correct".

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_select
documentation:
Return three values:
- a list of 3-tuples of (expression, (sql, params), alias)
- a klass_info structure,
- a dictionary of annotations

The (sql, params) is what the expression will produce, and alias is the
"AS alias" for the column (possibly None).

The klass_info structure contains the following information:
- The base model of the query.
- Which columns for that model are present in the query (by
  position of the select clause).
- related_klass_infos: [f, klass_info] to descent into

The annotations is a dictionary of {'attname': column position} values.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_order_by
documentation:
Return a list of 2-tuples of the form (expr, (sql, params, is_ref)) for
the ORDER BY clause.

The order_by clause can alter the select clause (for example it can add
aliases to clauses that do not yet have one, or it can add totally new
select clauses).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.quote_name_unless_alias
documentation:
A wrapper around connection.ops.quote_name that doesn't quote aliases
for table names. This avoids problems with some SQL dialects that treat
quoted strings specially (e.g. PostgreSQL).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.as_sql
documentation:
Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
parameters.

If 'with_limits' is False, any limit/offset information is not included
in the query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_default_columns
documentation:
Compute the default columns for selecting every field in the base
model. Will sometimes be called to pull in related models (e.g. via
select_related), in which case "opts" and "start_alias" will be given
to provide a starting point for the traversal.

Return a list of strings, quoted appropriately for use in SQL
directly, as well as a set of aliases used in the select statement (if
'as_pairs' is True, return a list of (alias, col_name) pairs instead
of strings as the first component and None as the second component).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_distinct
documentation:
Return a quoted list of fields to use in DISTINCT ON part of the query.

This method can alter the tables in the query, and thus it must be
called before get_from_clause().

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.find_ordering_name
documentation:
Return the table alias (the name might be ambiguous, the alias will
not be) and column name for ordering by the given 'name' parameter.
The 'name' is of the form 'field1__field2__...__fieldN'.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler._setup_joins
documentation:
Helper method for get_order_by() and get_distinct().

get_ordering() and get_distinct() must produce same target columns on
same input, as the prefixes of get_ordering() and get_distinct() must
match. Executing SQL where this is not true is an error.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_from_clause
documentation:
Return a list of strings that are joined together to go after the
"FROM" part of the query, as well as a list any extra parameters that
need to be included. Subclasses, can override this to create a
from-clause via a "select".

This should only be called after any SQL construction methods that
might change the tables that are needed. This means the select columns,
ordering, and distinct must be done first.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_related_selections
documentation:
Fill in the information needed for a select_related query. The current
depth is measured as the number of connections away from the root model
(for example, cur_depth=1 means we are looking at models with direct
connections to the root model).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.get_select_for_update_of_arguments
documentation:
Return a quoted list of arguments for the SELECT FOR UPDATE OF part of
the query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.results_iter
documentation: Return an iterator over the results from executing this query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.has_results
documentation:
Backends (e.g. NoSQL) can override this in order to use optimized
versions of "query has any results."

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler.execute_sql
documentation:
Run the query against the database and return the result(s). The
return value is a single data item if result_type is SINGLE, or an
iterator over the results if the result_type is MULTI.

result_type is either MULTI (use fetchmany() to retrieve all rows),
SINGLE (only retrieve a single row), or None. In this last case, the
cursor is returned if any query is executed, since it's used by
subclasses such as InsertQuery). It's possible, however, that no query
is needed, as the filters describe an empty set. In that case, None is
returned, to avoid any unnecessary database interaction.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLInsertCompiler.field_as_sql
documentation:
Take a field and a value intended to be saved on that field, and
return placeholder SQL and accompanying params. Check for raw values,
expressions, and fields with get_placeholder() defined in that order.

When field is None, consider the value raw and use it as the
placeholder, with no corresponding parameters returned.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLInsertCompiler.prepare_value
documentation:
Prepare a value to be used in a query by resolving it if it is an
expression and otherwise calling the field's get_db_prep_save().

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLInsertCompiler.pre_save_val
documentation:
Get the given field's value off the given obj. pre_save() is used for
things like auto_now on DateTimeField. Skip it if this is a raw query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLInsertCompiler.assemble_as_sql
documentation:
Take a sequence of N fields and a sequence of M rows of values, and
generate placeholder SQL and parameters for each field and value.
Return a pair containing:
 * a sequence of M rows of N SQL placeholder strings, and
 * a sequence of M rows of corresponding parameter values.

Each placeholder string may contain any number of '%s' interpolation
strings, and each parameter row will contain exactly as many params
as the total number of '%s's in the corresponding placeholder row.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLDeleteCompiler.as_sql
documentation:
Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
parameters.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLUpdateCompiler.as_sql
documentation:
Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
parameters.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLUpdateCompiler.execute_sql
documentation:
Execute the specified update. Return the number of rows affected by
the primary update query. The "primary update query" is the first
non-empty query that is executed. Row counts for any subsequent,
related queries are not available.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLUpdateCompiler.pre_sql_setup
documentation:
If the update depends on results from other tables, munge the "where"
conditions to match the format required for (portable) SQL updates.

If multiple updates are required, pull out the id values to update at
this point so that they don't change as a result of the progressive
updates.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.compiler.SQLAggregateCompiler.as_sql
documentation:
Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
parameters.

functions.name: plain.models.migrations.utils.resolve_relation
documentation:
Turn a model class or model reference string and return a model tuple.

package_label and model_name are used to resolve the scope of recursive and
unscoped model relationship.

functions.name: plain.models.migrations.utils.field_references
documentation:
Return either False or a FieldReference if `field` references provided
context.

False positives can be returned if `reference_field_name` is provided
without `reference_field` because of the introspection limitation it
incurs. This should not be an issue when this function is used to determine
whether or not an optimization can take place.

functions.name: plain.models.migrations.utils.get_references
documentation:
Generator of (model_state, name, field, reference) referencing
provided context.

If field_tuple is provided only references to this particular field of
model_tuple will be generated.

functions.name: plain.models.migrations.utils.field_is_referenced
documentation: Return whether `field_tuple` is referenced by any state models.

modules.name: plain.models.sql.query
documentation:
Create SQL statements for QuerySets.

The code in here encapsulates all of the SQL construction so that QuerySets
themselves do not have to (and could be backed by things other than SQL
databases). The abstraction barrier only works one way: this module has to know
all about the internals of models in order to get the information it needs.

functions.name: plain.models.sql.query.get_order_dir
documentation:
Return the field name and direction for an order specification. For
example, '-foo' is returned as ('foo', 'DESC').

The 'default' param is used to indicate which way no prefix (or a '+'
prefix) should sort. The '-' prefix always sorts the opposite way.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.query.RawQuery
documentation: A single raw SQL query.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.__str__
documentation:
Return the query as a string of SQL with the parameter values
substituted in (use sql_with_params() to see the unsubstituted string).

Parameter values won't necessarily be quoted correctly, since that is
done by the database interface at execution time.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.sql_with_params
documentation:
Return the query as an SQL string and the parameters that will be
substituted into the query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.__deepcopy__
documentation: Limit the amount of work when a Query is deepcopied.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.get_meta
documentation:
Return the Options instance (the model._meta) from which to start
processing. Normally, this is self.model._meta, but it can be changed
by subclasses.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clone
documentation:
Return a copy of the current Query. A lightweight alternative to
deepcopy().

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.chain
documentation:
Return a copy of the current Query that's ready for another operation.
The klass argument changes the type of the Query, e.g. UpdateQuery.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.get_aggregation
documentation: Return the dictionary with the values of the existing aggregations.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.get_count
documentation: Perform a COUNT() query using the current filter constraints.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.combine
documentation:
Merge the 'rhs' query into the current one (with any 'rhs' effects
being applied *after* (that is, "to the right of") anything in the
current query. 'rhs' is not modified during a call to this function.

The 'connector' parameter describes how to connect filters from the
'rhs' query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.get_select_mask
documentation:
Convert the self.deferred_loading data structure to an alternate data
structure, describing the field that *will* be loaded. This is used to
compute the columns to select from the database and also by the
QuerySet class to work out which fields are being initialized on each
model. Models that have all their fields included aren't mentioned in
the result, only those that have field restrictions in place.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.table_alias
documentation:
Return a table alias for the given table_name and whether this is a
new alias or not.

If 'create' is true, a new alias is always created. Otherwise, the
most recently created alias for the table (if one exists) is reused.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.ref_alias
documentation: Increases the reference count for this alias.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.unref_alias
documentation: Decreases the reference count for this alias.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.promote_joins
documentation:
Promote recursively the join type of given aliases and its children to
an outer join. If 'unconditional' is False, only promote the join if
it is nullable or the parent join is an outer join.

The children promotion is done to avoid join chains that contain a LOUTER
b INNER c. So, if we have currently a INNER b INNER c and a->b is promoted,
then we must also promote b->c automatically, or otherwise the promotion
of a->b doesn't actually change anything in the query results.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.demote_joins
documentation:
Change join type from LOUTER to INNER for all joins in aliases.

Similarly to promote_joins(), this method must ensure no join chains
containing first an outer, then an inner join are generated. If we
are demoting b->c join in chain a LOUTER b LOUTER c then we must
demote a->b automatically, or otherwise the demotion of b->c doesn't
actually change anything in the query results. .

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.reset_refcounts
documentation:
Reset reference counts for aliases so that they match the value passed
in `to_counts`.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.change_aliases
documentation:
Change the aliases in change_map (which maps old-alias -> new-alias),
relabelling any references to them in select columns and the where
clause.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.bump_prefix
documentation:
Change the alias prefix to the next letter in the alphabet in a way
that the other query's aliases and this query's aliases will not
conflict. Even tables that previously had no alias will get an alias
after this call. To prevent changing aliases use the exclude parameter.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.get_initial_alias
documentation:
Return the first alias for this query, after increasing its reference
count.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.count_active_tables
documentation:
Return the number of tables in this query with a non-zero reference
count. After execution, the reference counts are zeroed, so tables
added in compiler will not be seen by this method.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.join
documentation:
Return an alias for the 'join', either reusing an existing alias for
that join or creating a new one. 'join' is either a base_table_class or
join_class.

The 'reuse' parameter can be either None which means all joins are
reusable, or it can be a set containing the aliases that can be reused.

The 'reuse_with_filtered_relation' parameter is used when computing
FilteredRelation instances.

A join is always created as LOUTER if the lhs alias is LOUTER to make
sure chains like t1 LOUTER t2 INNER t3 aren't generated. All new
joins are created as LOUTER if the join is nullable.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.join_parent_model
documentation:
Make sure the given 'model' is joined in the query. If 'model' isn't
a parent of 'opts' or if it is None this method is a no-op.

The 'alias' is the root alias for starting the join, 'seen' is a dict
of model -> alias of existing joins. It must also contain a mapping
of None -> some alias. This will be returned in the no-op case.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_annotation
documentation: Add a single annotation expression to the Query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.solve_lookup_type
documentation: Solve the lookup type from the lookup (e.g.: 'foobar__id__icontains').

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.check_query_object_type
documentation:
Check whether the object passed while querying is of the correct type.
If not, raise a ValueError specifying the wrong object.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.check_related_objects
documentation: Check the type of object passed to query relations.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.check_filterable
documentation: Raise an error if expression cannot be used in a WHERE clause.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.build_lookup
documentation:
Try to extract transforms and lookup from given lhs.

The lhs value is something that works like SQLExpression.
The rhs value is what the lookup is going to compare against.
The lookups is a list of names to extract using get_lookup()
and get_transform().

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.try_transform
documentation:
Helper method for build_lookup(). Try to fetch and initialize
a transform for name parameter from lhs.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.build_filter
documentation:
Build a WhereNode for a single filter clause but don't add it
to this Query. Query.add_q() will then add this filter to the where
Node.

The 'branch_negated' tells us if the current branch contains any
negations. This will be used to determine if subqueries are needed.

The 'current_negated' is used to determine if the current filter is
negated or not and this will be used to determine if IS NULL filtering
is needed.

The difference between current_negated and branch_negated is that
branch_negated is set on first negation, but current_negated is
flipped for each negation.

Note that add_filter will not do any negating itself, that is done
upper in the code by add_q().

The 'can_reuse' is a set of reusable joins for multijoins.

If 'reuse_with_filtered_relation' is True, then only joins in can_reuse
will be reused.

The method will create a filter clause that can be added to the current
query. However, if the filter isn't added to the query then the caller
is responsible for unreffing the joins used.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_q
documentation:
A preprocessor for the internal _add_q(). Responsible for doing final
join promotion.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query._add_q
documentation: Add a Q-object to the current filter.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.build_filtered_relation_q
documentation: Add a FilteredRelation object to the current filter.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.names_to_path
documentation:
Walk the list of names and turns them into PathInfo tuples. A single
name in 'names' can generate multiple PathInfos (m2m, for example).

'names' is the path of names to travel, 'opts' is the model Options we
start the name resolving from, 'allow_many' is as for setup_joins().
If fail_on_missing is set to True, then a name that can't be resolved
will generate a FieldError.

Return a list of PathInfo tuples. In addition return the final field
(the last used join field) and target (which is a field guaranteed to
contain the same value as the final field). Finally, return those names
that weren't found (which are likely transforms and the final lookup).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.setup_joins
documentation:
Compute the necessary table joins for the passage through the fields
given in 'names'. 'opts' is the Options class for the current model
(which gives the table we are starting from), 'alias' is the alias for
the table to start the joining from.

The 'can_reuse' defines the reverse foreign key joins we can reuse. It
can be None in which case all joins are reusable or a set of aliases
that can be reused. Note that non-reverse foreign keys are always
reusable when using setup_joins().

The 'reuse_with_filtered_relation' can be used to force 'can_reuse'
parameter and force the relation on the given connections.

If 'allow_many' is False, then any reverse foreign key seen will
generate a MultiJoin exception.

Return the final field involved in the joins, the target field (used
for any 'where' constraint), the final 'opts' value, the joins, the
field path traveled to generate the joins, and a transform function
that takes a field and alias and is equivalent to `field.get_col(alias)`
in the simple case but wraps field transforms if they were included in
names.

The target field is the field containing the concrete value. Final
field can be something different, for example foreign key pointing to
that value. Final field is needed for example in some value
conversions (convert 'obj' in fk__id=obj to pk val using the foreign
key field for example).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.trim_joins
documentation:
The 'target' parameter is the final field being joined to, 'joins'
is the full list of join aliases. The 'path' contain the PathInfos
used to create the joins.

Return the final target field and table alias and the new active
joins.

Always trim any direct join if the target column is already in the
previous table. Can't trim reverse joins as it's unknown if there's
anything on the other side of the join.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.split_exclude
documentation:
When doing an exclude against any kind of N-to-many relation, we need
to use a subquery. This method constructs the nested query, given the
original exclude filter (filter_expr) and the portion up to the first
N-to-many relation field.

For example, if the origin filter is ~Q(child__name='foo'), filter_expr
is ('child__name', 'foo') and can_reuse is a set of joins usable for
filters in the original query.

We will turn this into equivalent of:
    WHERE NOT EXISTS(
        SELECT 1
        FROM child
        WHERE name = 'foo' AND child.parent_id = parent.id
        LIMIT 1
    )

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.set_limits
documentation:
Adjust the limits on the rows retrieved. Use low/high to set these,
as it makes it more Pythonic to read and write. When the SQL query is
created, convert them to the appropriate offset and limit values.

Apply any limits passed in here to the existing constraints. Add low
to the current low value and clamp both to any existing high value.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clear_limits
documentation: Clear any existing limits.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.can_filter
documentation:
Return True if adding filters to this instance is still possible.

Typically, this means no limits or offsets have been put on the results.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clear_select_clause
documentation: Remove all fields from SELECT clause.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clear_select_fields
documentation:
Clear the list of fields to select (but not extra_select columns).
Some queryset types completely replace any existing list of select
columns.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_distinct_fields
documentation: Add and resolve the given fields to the query's "distinct on" clause.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_fields
documentation:
Add the given (model) fields to the select set. Add the field names in
the order specified.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_ordering
documentation:
Add items from the 'ordering' sequence to the query's "order by"
clause. These items are either field names (not column names) --
possibly with a direction prefix ('-' or '?') -- or OrderBy
expressions.

If 'ordering' is empty, clear all ordering from the query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clear_ordering
documentation:
Remove any ordering settings if the current query allows it without
side effects, set 'force' to True to clear the ordering regardless.
If 'clear_default' is True, there will be no ordering in the resulting
query (not even the model's default).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.set_group_by
documentation:
Expand the GROUP BY clause required by the query.

This will usually be the set of all non-aggregate fields in the
return data. If the database backend supports grouping by the
primary key, and the query would be equivalent, the optimization
will be made automatically.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_select_related
documentation:
Set up the select_related data structure so that we only select
certain related models (as opposed to all models, when
self.select_related=True).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_extra
documentation:
Add data to the various extra_* attributes for user-created additions
to the query.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.clear_deferred_loading
documentation: Remove any fields from the deferred loading set.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_deferred_loading
documentation:
Add the given list of model field names to the set of fields to
exclude from loading from the database when automatic column selection
is done. Add the new field names to any existing field names that
are deferred (or removed from any existing field names that are marked
as the only ones for immediate loading).

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.add_immediate_loading
documentation:
Add the given list of model field names to the set of fields to
retrieve when the SQL is executed ("immediate loading" fields). The
field names replace any existing immediate loading field names. If
there are field names already specified for deferred loading, remove
those names from the new field_names before storing the new names
for immediate loading. (That is, immediate loading overrides any
existing immediate values, but respects existing deferrals.)

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.set_annotation_mask
documentation: Set the mask of annotations that will be returned by the SELECT.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.set_extra_mask
documentation:
Set the mask of extra select items that will be returned by SELECT.
Don't remove them from the Query since they might be used later.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.annotation_select
documentation:
Return the dictionary of aggregate columns that are not masked and
should be used in the SELECT clause. Cache this result for performance.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.trim_start
documentation:
Trim joins from the start of the join path. The candidates for trim
are the PathInfos in names_with_path structure that are m2m joins.

Also set the select column so the start matches the join.

This method is meant to be used for generating the subquery joins &
cols in split_exclude().

Return a lookup usable for doing outerq.filter(lookup=self) and a
boolean indicating if the joins in the prefix contain a LEFT OUTER join.
_

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.is_nullable
documentation:
Check if the given field should be treated as nullable.

Some backends treat '' as null and Plain treats such fields as
nullable for those backends. In such situations field.null can be
False even if we should treat the field as nullable.

class_variables.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.low_mark
documentation: Used for offset/limit.

class_variables.name: plain.models.sql.query.Query.high_mark
documentation: Used for offset/limit.
A single SQL query.

classes.name: plain.models.sql.query.JoinPromoter

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.JoinPromoter.add_votes
documentation:
Add single vote per item to self.votes. Parameter can be any
iterable.

methods.name: plain.models.sql.query.JoinPromoter.update_join_types
documentation:
Change join types so that the generated query is as efficient as
possible, but still correct. So, change as many joins as possible
to INNER, but don't make OUTER joins INNER if that could remove
results from the query.

A class to abstract away join promotion problems for complex filter
conditions.

classes.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector.changes
documentation:
Main entry point to produce a list of applicable changes.
Take a graph to base names on and an optional set of packages
to try and restrict to (restriction is not guaranteed)

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector.deep_deconstruct
documentation:
Recursive deconstruction for a field and its arguments.
Used for full comparison for rename/alter; sometimes a single-level
deconstruction will not compare correctly.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector.only_relation_agnostic_fields
documentation:
Return a definition of the fields that ignores field names and
what related fields actually relate to. Used for detecting renames (as
the related fields change during renames).

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector._detect_changes
documentation:
Return a dict of migration plans which will achieve the
change from from_state to to_state. The dict has app labels
as keys and a list of migrations as values.

The resulting migrations aren't specially named, but the names
do matter for dependencies inside the set.

convert_packages is the list of packages to convert to use migrations
(i.e. to make initial migrations for, in the usual case)

graph is an optional argument that, if provided, can help improve
dependency generation and avoid potential circular dependencies.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector._prepare_field_lists
documentation:
Prepare field lists and a list of the fields that used through models
in the old state so dependencies can be made from the through model
deletion to the field that uses it.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector._generate_through_model_map
documentation: Through model map generation.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.autodetector.MigrationAutodetector._build_migration_list
documentation:
Chop the lists of operations up into migrations with dependencies on
each other. Do this by going through an app's list of operations until
one is found that has an outgoing dependency that isn't in another
app's migration yet (hasn't been chopped off its list). Then chop off
the operations before it into a migration and move onto the next app.
If the loops completes without doing anything, there's a circular
dependency (which _should_ be impossible as the operations are
all split at this point so they can't depend and be depended on).

Take a pair of ProjectStates and compare them to see what the first would
need doing to make it match the second (the second usually being the
project's current state).

Note that this naturally operates on entire projects at a time,
as it's likely that changes interact (for example, you can't
add a ForeignKey without having a migration to add the table it
depends on first). A user interface may offer single-app usage
if it wishes, with the caveat that it may not always be possible.

documentation:
Return either a list of operations the actual operation should be

Base class for migration operations.

It's responsible for both mutating the in-memory model state
(see db/migrations/state.py) to represent what it performs, as well
as actually performing it against a live database.

Note that some operations won't modify memory state at all (e.g. data
copying operations), and some will need their modifications to be
optionally specified by the user (e.g. custom Python code snippets)

Due to the way this class deals with deconstruction, it should be
considered immutable.

classes.name: plain.models.migrations.optimizer.MigrationOptimizer

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.optimizer.MigrationOptimizer.optimize
documentation:
Main optimization entry point. Pass in a list of Operation instances,
get out a new list of Operation instances.

Unfortunately, due to the scope of the optimization (two combinable
operations might be separated by several hundred others), this can't be
done as a peephole optimization with checks/output implemented on
the Operations themselves; instead, the optimizer looks at each
individual operation and scans forwards in the list to see if there
are any matches, stopping at boundaries - operations which can't
be optimized over (RunSQL, operations on the same field/model, etc.)

The inner loop is run until the starting list is the same as the result
list, and then the result is returned. This means that operation
optimization must be stable and always return an equal or shorter list.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.optimizer.MigrationOptimizer.optimize_inner
documentation: Inner optimization loop.

Power the optimization process, where you provide a list of Operations
and you are returned a list of equal or shorter length - operations
are merged into one if possible.

For example, a CreateModel and an AddField can be optimized into a
new CreateModel, and CreateModel and DeleteModel can be optimized into
nothing.

creation, it's the one thing we can't do normally via migrations.
We manually handle table creation/schema updating (using schema backend)
and then have a floating model to do queries with.

If a migration is unapplied its row is removed from the table. Having
a row in the table always means a migration is applied.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.graph.MigrationGraph.leaf_nodes

gets handled further up, in the interactive command - it's usually the
result of a VCS merge and needs some user input.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.graph.MigrationGraph.make_state
documentation:
Given a migration node or nodes, return a complete ProjectState for it.
If at_end is False, return the state before the migration has run.
If nodes is not provided, return the overall most current project state.

Represent the digraph of all migrations in a project.

Each migration is a node, and each dependency is an edge. There are
no implicit dependencies between numbered migrations - the numbering is
merely a convention to aid file listing. Every new numbered migration
has a declared dependency to the previous number, meaning that VCS
branch merges can be detected and resolved.

Migrations files can be marked as replacing another set of migrations -
this is to support the "squash" feature. The graph handler isn't responsible
for these; instead, the code to load them in here should examine the
migration files and if the replaced migrations are all either unapplied
or not present, it should ignore the replaced ones, load in just the
replacing migration, and repoint any dependencies that pointed to the
replaced migrations to point to the replacing one.

A node should be a tuple: (app_path, migration_name). The tree special-cases
things within an app - namely, root nodes and leaf nodes ignore dependencies
to other packages.

documentation:

Load migration files from disk and their status from the database.

Migration files are expected to live in the "migrations" directory of
an app. Their names are entirely unimportant from a code perspective,
but will probably follow the 1234_name.py convention.

On initialization, this class will scan those directories, and open and
read the Python files, looking for a class called Migration, which should
inherit from plain.models.migrations.Migration. See
plain.models.migrations.migration for what that looks like.

Some migrations will be marked as "replacing" another set of migrations.
These are loaded into a separate set of migrations away from the main ones.
If all the migrations they replace are either unapplied or missing from
disk, then they are injected into the main set, replacing the named migrations.
Any dependency pointers to the replaced migrations are re-pointed to the
new migration.

This does mean that this class MUST also talk to the database as well as
to disk, but this is probably fine. We're already not just operating
in memory.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.migration.Migration.unapply
documentation:
Take a project_state representing all migrations prior to this one
and a schema_editor for a live database and apply the migration
in a reverse order.

The backwards migration process consists of two phases:

1. The intermediate states from right before the first until right
   after the last operation inside this migration are preserved.
2. The operations are applied in reverse order using the states
   recorded in step 1.

methods.name: plain.models.migrations.migration.Migration.suggest_name
documentation:
Suggest a name for the operations this migration might represent. Names
are not guaranteed to be unique, but put some effort into the fallback
name to avoid VCS conflicts if possible.

The base class for all migrations.

Migration files will import this from plain.models.migrations.Migration
and subclass it as a class called Migration. It will have one or more
of the following attributes:

 - operations: A list of Operation instances, probably from
   plain.models.migrations.operations
 - dependencies: A list of tuples of (app_path, migration_name)
 - run_before: A list of tuples of (app_path, migration_name)
 - replaces: A list of migration_names

Note that all migrations come out of migrations and into the Loader or
Graph as instances, having been initialized with their app label and name.

def serialize_db_to_string(self):
Serialize all data in the database into a JSON string.
Designed only for test runner usage; will not handle large
amounts of data.
# Iteratively return every object for all models to serialize.
def get_objects():
from plain.models.migrations.loader import MigrationLoader
loader = MigrationLoader(self.connection)
for package_config in packages.get_package_configs():
if (
package_config.models_module is not None
and package_config.label in loader.migrated_packages
for model in package_config.get_models():
if model._meta.can_migrate(
self.connection
) and router.allow_migrate_model(self.connection.alias, model):
queryset = model._base_manager.using(
self.connection.alias,
).order_by(model._meta.pk.name)
yield from queryset.iterator()
# Serialize to a string
out = StringIO()
serializers.serialize("json", get_objects(), indent=None, stream=out)
return out.getvalue()
def deserialize_db_from_string(self, data):
Reload the database with data from a string generated by
the serialize_db_to_string() method.
data = StringIO(data)
table_names = set()
# Load data in a transaction to handle forward references and cycles.
with atomic(using=self.connection.alias):
# Disable constraint checks, because some databases (MySQL) doesn't
# support deferred checks.
with self.connection.constraint_checks_disabled():
for obj in serializers.deserialize(
"json", data, using=self.connection.alias
obj.save()
table_names.add(obj.object.__class__._meta.db_table)
# Manually check for any invalid keys that might have been added,
# because constraint checks were disabled.
self.connection.check_constraints(table_names=table_names)
